question.text: Under the SEBI (FPI) Regulations, 2019, the purchase of equity shares of each company by a single FPI including its investor group shall be:

answer.text: Below 5 percent of the total paid-up equity capital on a fully diluted basis
Below 10 percent of the total paid-up equity capital on a fully diluted basis
Below 15 percent of the total paid-up equity capital on a fully diluted basis
Below 20 percent of the total paid-up equity capital on a fully diluted basis

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 20(6) clearly states that the purchase of equity shares by a single FPI including its investor group shall be below 10% of the total paid-up equity capital on a fully diluted basis. **The Trap:** 5% is the SAST disclosure threshold, not the FPI investment limit. Candidates often confuse these two thresholds. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) and NISM Series XV (Research Analyst) also reference this 10% limit. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 10% threshold is a hard line - crossing it converts the FPI investment into FDI.
